原文:

This week I got the opportunity to improve the developer contribution documentation for the Telescope project.

Overview

When I was working on a fix for this issue locally on my machine, I ran into a problem where I was unable to see any blog posts, even though I used the environment-setup file as my guide to setup Telescope. Since the bug I was trying to fix did not require access to blog feeds, I filed a separate issue on GitHub.

The Problem

The problem was that the environment-setup file was missing information on properly seeding the database utilized by Telescope. The needed information was in a separate file.

The Goal

My goal was to enhance the developer experience by correctly integrating all the information related to running Telescope in one file - environment-setup.

information Added

The Outcome

Users can easily navigate to different files to initialize the database. All the steps are in one file

Thank you Josue, for your help and support.

item.title: 什么是 Node.js？ | What is Node.js?
item.description:
一种向网页添加交互性的工具。 长期以来，它的用例仅限于浏览器，但 Node.js 的创建完全改变了它在现代 Web 开发世界中的角色。

其充满活力的社区创建了许多工具，为服务器、台式机和嵌入式系统上的许多应用程序提供支持。

  • 它在生产中用于为程序员最喜欢的编辑器 VSCode 和 Microsoft Teams 等消息传递应用程序提供动力。
  • 嵌入式系统可以利用 low.js 来开发应用程序。

为什么选择 Node.js？

在 Node.js 之前

与流行的看法相反，Node 的发明并不是为了允许在浏览器之外使用 JavaScript。 后者是一个积极的结果。 开发它是为了创建性能更高的 Web 服务器。

十年前，处理多个用户连接需要创建一个新线程，即 Web 服务器进程中的一个独立执行单元。 这种方法隐含两个问题：

  • 如果您执行数据库查询，它会阻止整个线程执行其他操作，直到它收到结果。

  • 处理与相关线程的并发连接需要高内存消耗并且增加管理它们之间的同步的难度。

在上面的代码片段中，Node.js 可以处理并发连接并运行其他代码，因为数据库查询执行委托给事件循环。 数据库操作有一个回调函数，当事件循环通知单个线程查询完成时运行。

要了解 Node.js，我们需要了解 V8。

并非偶然，Node.js 是一个 C++ 程序，它集成了 V8 引擎和其他库来丰富 JavaScript 的新特性：

  • 结论

您将在下一篇文章中更详细地探索事件循环。

如果您喜欢这篇文章，请在 Twitter 上关注我们@fabrizio.lallo和@AndrewHu368



原文:

JavaScript had the reputation of being a toy language. A tool to add interactivity to web pages. For a long time, its use cases were limited to the browser, but the creation of Node.js completely changed its role in the modern web development world.

Node.js allows us to run JavaScript outside the browser, and out of nowhere, a limited language became the favorite companion for thousands of developers working on small and big projects.

Its vibrant community created many tools that power many applications on servers, desktops, and embedded systems.

  • Node.js is commonly used with Express or Fastify to power complex backend services.
  • Electron package Node.js and Chrome to create cross-platform desktop applications. It is used in production to power programmers’ favorite editor VSCode and messaging applications like Microsoft Teams.
  • Embedded systems can leverage low.js to develop applications.

Why Node.js?

Before Node.js

Contrary to popular belief, Node wasn’t invented to allow JavaScript outside the browser. The latter was a positive outcome. It was developed to create more performant web servers.

Ten years ago, handling multiple user connections involved the creation of a new thread, an independent unit of execution within the web server process. This methodology implied two problems:

  • If you perform a database query, it blocks the entire thread from executing other operations until it receives the result.

  • Handling concurrent connections with associated threads requires high memory consumption and increased difficulty in managing synchronization between them.

The Node.js way

JavaScript and Node.js solve the problem of threads' high memory usage and synchronization by using a single thread paired with an event loop mechanism.

In the code snippet above, Node.js can handle concurrent connections and run other pieces of code because the database query execution is delegated to the event loop. The database operation has a callback function which is run when the event loop notifies the single thread that the query is completed.

Node.js internal overview

To understand Node.js, we need to understand V8.

V8 is a C++ program that runs JavaScript code and converts it into instructions understandable by the computer microprocessor. V8 is included in Google Chrome and other browsers to run our websites JavaScript and enable our rich web experience.

The V8 engine is not limited to modern browsers but can also be embedded into any C++ program and extended with more functionalities. Not by chance, Node.js is a C++ program that integrates the V8 engine and other libraries to enrich JavaScript with new features:

  • libuv, the C++ library responsible for the event loop, TCP and UDP connection sockets, DNS resolution, file system operations, data encryption, etc.
  • C++ bindings are responsible for invoking C++ code when running Node.js-specific JavaScript code.
  • The JavaScript core API.

Conclusion

Node.js clever design and event loop has empowered thousands of developers and products to create web servers capable of handling thousands of concurrent connections without the hassle of threads’ high memory consumption and synchronization. Node.js event loop capabilities are not limited to database queries and concurrent connections, but they are also used in production to process large files, encrypt/decrypt data, perform network requests, etc.

You will explore in more detail the event loop in the following article.

If you liked the article, follow us on Twitter @fabrizio.lallo and @AndrewHu368

item.title: GNU | GNU
item.description:
发表于有没有想过为什么我们看不到 Windows 或 MAC OS 的源代码？ 为什么我们在购买笔记本电脑或 PC 时要为操作系统付费？ 为什么我们可以免费安装 Linux 操作系统，但需要为 Windows 付费？ 因为这些是受版权保护的闭源程序，无法进一步研究或使用。 您可能还听说过或使用过 UNIX OS，它是一个封闭源代码。

需要一个开源操作系统，一种免费软件，其代码可以免费获得，可以修改和分发。 免费并不一定意味着免费，这里免费代表免费使用。 免费软件


因此，GNU！

但是 GNU 到底是什么？

出于任何目的自由运行程序

  • 自由研究程序的机制和修改它
  • 重新分发副本的自由
  • 自由改进和更改修改版本以供公众使用

    • 为了实现这个宣言，甚至操作系统也需要是自由软件。
    这就是开发 GNU/Linux 的原因！

    这意味着它类似于 UNIX，但它是免费软件，并且不包含类似于 UNIX 的代码。 大多数时候 GNU 也称为 GNU/Linux 或简称为 Linux，因为它使用类似 UNIX 的内核 Linux。


    为什么 GNU 与 Linux 一起出现？
    但它不能按原样使用。 它需要一个操作系统来解释计算机。 因此，它与类 Unix 的开源内核 Linux 相结合。 这些统称为 GNU/Linux 或 Linux 或 GNU。

    通过阅读和接受意识形态，你也是其中的一部分:)。

    多逗弄你的大脑。 从一点到字节……

    • 为什么我们需要开源代码？ 我们不能只使用许可版本吗？

      阅读：关于开源的博客，开源

    • 我对 Windows 太熟悉了，那么为什么选择 Linux 呢？

      阅读： Linux

    原文:

    COEP's Free Software Users Group

    Posted on Oct 30

    Ever wondered why can’t we see the source code of Windows or MAC OS? Why do we pay for the operating system when buying a laptop or PC? Why we can install Linux OS freely but need to pay for Windows? Windows, MAC, Unix: You use them daily but don’t know how they actually work. Because these are closed-source programs that are copyrighted and not available for further research or use. You may have also heard of or used the UNIX OS which is a closed source.

    There was a need for an open-source OS, a freeware whose code is freely available and can be modified and distributed. Free doesn’t necessarily mean free of cost, here free stands for free use. Free Software


    Hence, GNU!

    But what exactly is GNU?

    The GNU Project GNU Project was announced by Richard Stallman in 1983. The aim was to develop and publish software collaboratively and allow users to use them freely, copy and distribute.

    The GNU Manifesto:

    • Freedom to run a program for any purpose
    • Freedom to study the mechanics of the program and modify it
    • Freedom to redistribute copies
    • Freedom to improve and change modified versions for public use

    To implement this manifesto, even the operating system needed to be free software.
    That’s why GNU/Linux was developed!

    The GNU Operating System
    GNU stands for GNU’s Not Unix! Meaning it is UNIX-like but is free software and does not contain UNIX-like code. GNU is licensed by GNU General Public License (GNU GPL).

    GNU is a collection of free softwares which can be used as an operating system or can be used in parts with other operating systems.
    Most of the time GNU is also referred to as GNU/Linux or simply Linux, as it uses a UNIX-like kernel Linux.


    Tux: The logo of Linux

    Why does GNU come with Linux?
    GNU is just the source code or the software developed under the GPL. But it cannot be used as it is. It requires an OS to interpret the computer. So, it is combined with the Unix-like Open Source Kernel, Linux. These together are popularly known as GNU/Linux or Linux or GNU.

    GNU not only developed an operating system, but it also grew a worldwide open-source community! And by reading and accepting the ideology, you are a part too :).

    Tease your brain more. Go from a bit to the byte…

    • Why do we need open-source code? Can’t we just work with licensed versions?

      Read: Blog on Open Source, Open Source

    • I am too comfortable with Windows, so why Linux?

      Read: Linux

      ~ Vaishnavi Pabalkar

item.title: 如何将 Giscus 评论添加到 Docusaurus | How to add Giscus comments to Docusaurus
item.description:
这篇文章分步描述了如何将Giscus （由 GitHub Discussions 提供支持的评论系统）添加到 Docusaurus 生成的静态网站。

设置 Giscus

按照当前章节中的步骤设置 Giscus 并将其连接到 GitHub 讨论。

启用 GitHub 讨论

  • 在您的 Github 帐户中创建 Github 存储库，其中评论存储在讨论部分。
  • 在创建的存储库的主页中，转到设置。
  • 在“功能”部分下，单击“设置讨论”。
  • 在“开始新讨论”中编辑模板，然后单击“开始讨论”。

启用 Giscus

  • 在您的 GitHub 帐户中配置giscus。
  • 在“存储库访问”部分中，仅添加上一步创建的存储库以供 giscus 访问，然后单击“保存”。

获取存储库 API 密钥

  • 在GraphQL API Explorer中使用 GitHub 帐户登录。
  • 使用以下查询获取已创建存储库的 id、讨论类别及其详细信息（例如 id 和名称）。 笔记！ 将owner和name替换为您创建的 GitHub 帐户名称和存储库名称。
 query { repository(owner: "nameOfYourGitHubAccount" , name: "nameOfCreatedRepository" ) { id discussionCategories(first: 10 ) { edges { node { id name } } } }
}

结果 json 对象将类似于：

 { "data" : { "repository" : { "id" : "R_kgDOIVqhTg" , "discussionCategories" : { "edges" : [ { "node" : { "id" : "DIC_kwDOIVqhTs4CSSES" , "name" : "Announcements" } }, { "node" : { "id" : "DIC_kwDOIVqhTs4CSSET" , "name" : "General" } }, { "node" : { "id" : "DIC_kwDOIVqhTs4CSSEV" , "name" : "Ideas" } }, { "node" : { "id" : "DIC_kwDOIVqhTs4CSSEX" , "name" : "Polls" } }, { "node" : { "id" : "DIC_kwDOIVqhTs4CSSEU" , "name" : "Q&A" } }, { "node" : { "id" : "DIC_kwDOIVqhTs4CSSEW" , "name" : "Show and tell" } } ] } } }
}

创建 Giscus 组件

  • 安装 @giscus/react 包
  • 创建 Giscus 组件，例如在/src/components/GiscusComponent ，如下所示：
 import React from ' react ' ; import Giscus from " @giscus/react " ; import { useColorMode } from ' @docusaurus/theme-common ' ; export default function GiscusComponent () { const { colorMode } = useColorMode (); return ( < Giscus repo = " nameOfYourGitHubAccount/nameOfCreatedRepository " repoId = " idOfCreatedRepo " category = " General " categoryId = " IdOfDiscussionCategory " // Eg id of "General" mapping = " url " // Important! To map comments to URL term = " Welcome to @giscus/react component! " strict = " 0 " reactionsEnabled = " 1 " emitMetadata = " 1 " inputPosition = " top " theme = { colorMode } lang = " en " loading = " lazy " crossorigin = " anonymous " async / > ); }

创建 BlogPostItemWrapper 包装器

  • 创建 BlogPostItemWrapper 包装器以使用 Giscus 评论系统包装博客文章，如下所示：
 npm run swizzle [ theme name] [ component name] -- --wrap # Example: npm run swizzle @docusaurus/theme-classic BlogPostItemWrapper -- --wrap

这将在下创建一个 BlogPostItemWrapper 包装器
编辑index.js如下：

 import React from ' react ' ; import { useBlogPost } from ' @docusaurus/theme-common/internal ' import BlogPostItem from ' @theme-original/BlogPostItem ' ; import GiscusComponent from ' @site/src/components/GiscusComponent ' ; import useIsBrowser from ' @docusaurus/useIsBrowser ' ; export default function BlogPostItemWrapper ( props ) { const { metadata } = useBlogPost () const isBrowser = useIsBrowser (); const { frontMatter , slug , title } = metadata const { enableComments } = frontMatter // Prevent error during build (see https://docusaurus.io/docs/advanced/ssg for the Error 'ReferenceError: window is not defined') var isCurrentUrlBlog = false if ( isBrowser ) { isCurrentUrlBlog = window . location . pathname === " /blog " } return ( <> < BlogPostItem {... props } / > {( enableComments && ! isCurrentUrlBlog ) && ( < GiscusComponent /> )} < / > ); }

请注意，在 BlogPostItemWrapper 中使用useBlogPost() Hook 来激活每个博客文章的评论可配置。 必须将键值enableComments: true添加到您的博客文章md文件中才能激活评论。 例如：

 --- title: "Title of blog post" authors: author tags: [ keywordOne, keywordTwo] enableComments: true # for Gisqus ---

参考

使用 Giscus 将评论系统添加到您的静态站点

一旦暂停，m19v 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，m19v 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，m19v 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 m19v 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 m19v 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 m19v 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

This post is a step-by-step description of how to add Giscus, a commenting system powered by GitHub Discussions, to a static website generated by Docusaurus.

Setup Giscus

Follow the steps in the sections of the current chapter to set up Giscus and connect it to the GitHub discussions.

Enable GitHub discussion

  • Create Github repository in your Github account where comments are stored in Discussion section.
  • In the main page of created repository go to Settings.
  • Under "Features" section, click "Set up discussions".
  • Edit the template in "Start a new discussion" and click "Start discussion".

Enable Giscus

  • Configure giscus in your GitHub account.
  • In Section "Repository access" add only created repository from previous step to be accessed by giscus and click "Save".

Get repository API key

  • Login with GitHub account in GraphQL API Explorer.
  • Use following query to fetch the id of created repository, discussion categories with its details (e.g. id and name). Note! Replace owner and name with your GitHub account name and name of repository you created. 
query { repository(owner: "nameOfYourGitHubAccount", name:"nameOfCreatedRepository"){ id discussionCategories(first:10) { edges { node { id name } } } }
}

The result json object will be similar to:

{ "data": { "repository": { "id": "R_kgDOIVqhTg", "discussionCategories": { "edges": [ { "node": { "id": "DIC_kwDOIVqhTs4CSSES", "name": "Announcements" } }, { "node": { "id": "DIC_kwDOIVqhTs4CSSET", "name": "General" } }, { "node": { "id": "DIC_kwDOIVqhTs4CSSEV", "name": "Ideas" } }, { "node": { "id": "DIC_kwDOIVqhTs4CSSEX", "name": "Polls" } }, { "node": { "id": "DIC_kwDOIVqhTs4CSSEU", "name": "Q&A" } }, { "node": { "id": "DIC_kwDOIVqhTs4CSSEW", "name": "Show and tell" } } ] } } }
}

Create Giscus component

  • Install @giscus/react package
  • Create Giscus Component, e.g. under /src/components/GiscusComponent as follows: 
import React from 'react';
import Giscus from "@giscus/react";
import { useColorMode } from '@docusaurus/theme-common'; export default function GiscusComponent() { const { colorMode } = useColorMode(); return ( <Giscus repo="nameOfYourGitHubAccount/nameOfCreatedRepository" repoId="idOfCreatedRepo" category="General" categoryId="IdOfDiscussionCategory" // E.g. id of "General" mapping="url" // Important! To map comments to URL term="Welcome to @giscus/react component!" strict="0" reactionsEnabled="1" emitMetadata="1" inputPosition="top" theme={colorMode} lang="en" loading="lazy" crossorigin="anonymous" async />
 );
}

Create BlogPostItemWrapper wrapper

  • Create BlogPostItemWrapper wrapper to wrap blog posts with Giscus commenting system as follows: 
npm run swizzle [theme name] [component name] -- --wrap # Example:
npm run swizzle @docusaurus/theme-classic BlogPostItemWrapper -- --wrap

This will create a BlogPostItemWrapper wrapper under
src/theme. Edit index.js as follows:

import React from 'react';
import { useBlogPost } from '@docusaurus/theme-common/internal'
import BlogPostItem from '@theme-original/BlogPostItem';
import GiscusComponent from '@site/src/components/GiscusComponent';
import useIsBrowser from '@docusaurus/useIsBrowser'; export default function BlogPostItemWrapper(props) { const { metadata } = useBlogPost() const isBrowser = useIsBrowser(); const { frontMatter, slug, title } = metadata const { enableComments } = frontMatter // Prevent error during build (see https://docusaurus.io/docs/advanced/ssg for the Error 'ReferenceError: window is not defined') var isCurrentUrlBlog = false if (isBrowser) { isCurrentUrlBlog = window.location.pathname === "/blog" } return ( <> <BlogPostItem {...props} />
 {(enableComments && !isCurrentUrlBlog) && ( <GiscusComponent /> )} </>
 );
}

Note that the useBlogPost() Hook is used in BlogPostItemWrapper to make activation of comments per blog post configurable. The key value enableComments: true must be added to your blog post md file in order to activate commenting for it. E.g.:

---
title: "Title of blog post"
authors: author
tags: [keywordOne, keywordTwo]
enableComments: true # for Gisqus
---

References

giscus.app

graphql.org

Add comment system to your static site with Giscus

Lars Gyrup Brink Nielsen - Aug 21
